page: OVHcloud r2-120 is a memory-optimized server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel processor (Haswell microarchitecture, without TSX) operating at 2.2 GHz. The configuration delivers 8 dedicated physical cores and 8 vCPUs, meaning there is no hyperthreading enabled with 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 120.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 15.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 200 GB SSD, and the network interface provides a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a clear tradeoff between memory capacity and raw processing speed. The older Intel Haswell CPU architecture results in poor single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core and Geekbench single-core scores falling into the bottom 10% of tested servers. Multi-core CPU performance is also weak, placing in the bottom 25%. However, memory subsystem performance is more balanced; cached small-block read bandwidth averages 582.18 GB/sec, though uncached large-block write bandwidth drops to a poor 12.56 GB/sec. Despite the older CPU, the system achieves average results in Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations, processing 8.58 million operations per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r2-120 offers a high density of memory relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require modern CPU instruction sets like AVX-512. The server is appropriate for hosting medium-sized databases, caching layers such as Redis, and memory-intensive web serving applications. It is less appropriate for CPU-heavy tasks, software compilation, or modern LLM inference, where its older processor architecture and lack of GPU acceleration present significant performance bottlenecks.
description: OVHcloud r2-120 is a memory-optimized server instance designed for memory-intensive workloads. Running on the KVM hypervisor, it features 8 dedicated x86_64 Intel cores (Haswell architecture) clocked at 2.2 GHz, 120.0 GB of RAM, a 200 GB local SSD, and a 1 Gbps network baseline. While the older CPU architecture results in poor single-core and weak multi-core benchmark performance, the instance maintains average capabilities in memory-bound operations. It achieves average performance in Passmark database tests and Redis SET operations, handling 8.58 million operations per second. The high memory-to-core ratio of 15.0 GB per core makes this server qualitatively cost-effective for memory-heavy applications that do not demand intensive compute. It is appropriate for caching layers, database hosting, and general memory-bound utility workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud r2-120 is a memory-optimized KVM instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel cores, 120 GB RAM, and a 200 GB SSD, offering balanced database and caching performance despite older CPU architecture.
meta_description: OVHcloud r2-120 is a memory-optimized server with 8 dedicated Intel cores, 120 GB RAM, and a 200 GB SSD, configured for caching and database workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring dedicated resources and local SSD storage, optimized for caching and database workloads.
